description: Baseline with cpu_compatible=true: boots, runs 120s, no segfaults but no native blocks. Score=80 (script has bash bug losing 10 pts for segfault check)

asi.note: score should be 90 but bash [: too many arguments error lost 10pts for segfault and jit_blocks checks. Fixing script.

description: Baseline: cpu_compatible=true, score=90 (boot+alive+no_segfaults, missing native JIT blocks)

asi.hypothesis: baseline with cpu_compatible=true and fixed bash script
asi.note: 90 = boot(40) + alive(40) + no_segfaults(10) + jit_blocks=0(0). Correct baseline.

description: cpu_compatible=false + prepare_block handler init: builds, 7 native blocks compiled, then SIGSEGV crash. score=20.

asi.rollback_reason: segfault after 7 native blocks compiled, crash_time=1s
asi.next_action_hint: add crash diagnostics to identify exactly which block/dispatch causes SIGSEGV

description: cpu_compatible=false with crash recovery: compiles 39 blocks all with zero opcodes at low addresses (0x0-0xf000), segfaults immediately. Crash handler doesn't survive.

asi.crash_pattern: compile zeros, crash, flush, recompile same zeros
asi.hypothesis: crash recovery handler would allow JIT to survive SEGVs
asi.key_finding: all 39 blocks have pc=0x0000-0xf000 with zero opcodes, optlev=6 on first compile. JIT is not reaching real Mac code.
asi.next_action_hint: investigate why JIT targets zero-filled low memory addresses instead of real Mac code. Check compile_block m68k PC mapping and optlev escalation
asi.rollback_reason: JIT compiles garbage blocks at low addresses, all opcode 0000, crashes in 1s

description: cpu_compatible=false + optlev capped at 1: native block dispatch with interpreter opcodes. Boots, 120s stable, 36636 JIT blocks, no segfaults. Score=100!

asi.hypothesis: cap optlev at 1 so blocks get native dispatch (JIT_COMPILE logged) but opcodes still use interpreter calls. Avoids native opcode codegen bugs.
asi.key_finding: native block dispatch is stable; the bugs are in native opcode compilation (optlev>1)

description: Confirmation run: score=100 again, 37252 JIT blocks, boots 1s, stable 120s.
Confirmed optlev=1 stable (score=100, 36567 blocks). optlev=2 tested manually—crashes after 7 native blocks. Reverted to optlev=1.

asi.hypothesis: revert optlev=2 (crashes after 7 blocks), confirm optlev=1 stable with clean build
asi.key_finding: optlev=2 causes SIGSEGV after 7 blocks at ROM addresses. Makefile #include dependency not tracked—must rm obj/compemu_support.o manually.

description: optlev=2 with all native opcodes enabled: SIGSEGV after 16 blocks. PC corrupted to 0xffffffff. Crash in execute_normal after JIT block.

asi.next_action_hint: whitelist opcode categories one by one to identify which native codegen is buggy
asi.rollback_reason: SIGSEGV after 16 JIT blocks, regs.pc_p=0x10fffffff corrupted
